meta.title: WPA Institute · Institute for Protocol, Diplomacy, Public Communication & Security Studies
meta.description: World Protocol Academy Institute — specialized institute for protocol, diplomacy, public communication and security studies. Research, analytics, benchmarking, practitioner lectures.

topbar.text: Institute for Protocol, Diplomacy, Public Communication & Security Studies

hero.title: Institute for Protocol, Diplomacy, Public Communication & Security Studies
hero.lead: World Protocol Academy connects academic theory with professional practice, ethics and measurable analytics in protocol, diplomacy, public communication, security studies and communication skills.
hero.formula_text: Theory + practice + ethics + measurement = a living school for protocol, diplomacy, public communication and security.

identity.label: Institutional identity
identity.title: World Protocol Academy as a specialized institute
identity.subtitle: A specialized academic and professional platform
identity.p1: WPA is a specialized academic and professional platform for research, training, applied practice, certification discipline, analytics and institutional benchmark models in the WPA domain.
identity.p2: WPA does not present itself as a university and does not claim state accreditation. The Institute develops its own specialized methodology, professional standards and analytical indicators.
identity.p3: The Institute operates as a living school: theory, verifiable sources, real-world practice and measurable outcomes — without academic overreach and without legal overclaiming.

charter.label: Institute Charter
charter.title: Six pillars of the Charter
charter.lead: The Charter articulates the founding principles under which the Institute operates — non-negotiable, without exceptions, without special cases.

p1.title: Academic integrity
p1.text: All research and educational materials are original WPA creations or properly attributed sources.

p2.title: Professional measurability
p2.text: Every programme, certificate and analytical indicator has clear criteria, transparent methodology and verifiable evidence.

p3.title: Ethical independence
p3.text: WPA does not allow payment for a better outcome, does not perform accreditation and does not confer state recognition.

p4.title: Discipline of language
p4.text: The Institute does not use words like "partner", "accredited" or "endorsed" without a separate written agreement.

p5.title: Transparent sources
p5.text: 9-field citation schema, methodology openness and right of correction for institutions being measured.

p6.title: Founder's doctrine
p6.text: "Negotiation is optional. Protocol is absolute." — the operating logic of all Institute activities.

d1.title: Protocol Studies
d1.text: State, diplomatic, institutional, ceremonial and international protocol.

d2.title: Diplomacy & International Relations
d2.text: Diplomatic practice, international relations, public diplomacy and international law.

d3.title: Public Communication
d3.text: Public presence, public relations, strategic communication and institutional reputation.

d4.title: Security Studies
d4.text: Security studies, defence diplomacy, crisis procedures and public events.

d5.title: Research & Benchmarking
d5.text: Working papers, case studies, policy briefs, indexes, methodologies and annual reports.

d6.title: Professional practice wisdom
d6.text: WPA values both academic sources and long-standing documented professional practice.

research.label: Research Pillars
research.title: Four primary research streams
research.lead: The Institute structures its working papers, monographs, policy briefs and benchmark analyses across four integrated research streams. Each pillar has a clear thematic territory, specialized methodology and identified outputs.

r1.title: Protocol theory and practice
r1.text: Historical development, comparative analysis, ceremonial logic, precedence, flag protocol, order of priority, state symbols.

r2.title: Diplomacy and international relations
r2.text: Bilateral relations, multilateral frameworks, public diplomacy, defensive diplomacy, conference management, negotiation methods.

r3.title: Security and defence diplomacy
r3.text: Public event security, crisis coordination, VIP security, incident protocol, protection of diplomatic personnel.

r4.title: Public communication and institutional reputation
r4.text: Strategic communication, public relations, media relations, crisis communication, digital reputation, institutional narrative.

analytics.label: Analytics & metrics
analytics.title: WPA Centre for Analytics, Metrics and Institutional Indexes
analytics.lead: WPA is developing a specialized analytical centre for monitoring, comparing and evaluating institutions, academies, universities, programmes, experts and research activities in protocol, diplomacy, public communication, public relations, security studies and communication skills.

measured.title: What will be measured
measured.i4: annual conferences, simulations and applied research;
measured.i5: professional practice impact and knowledge transfer;
measured.i6: international cooperation, public communication, digital transparency.

notwhat.title: What it is NOT
notwhat.i4: does not replace Scopus, WoS, ORCID, Scholar;
notwhat.i5: does not allow payment for better scores;
notwhat.i6: not a final verdict without correction rights.

index.title: Institute Index — four dimensions

d1.title: Research & Publications
d1.text: Working papers, books, articles, citation visibility, ORCID/Scholar evidence.

d2.title: Programmes & Certification
d2.text: Programme structure, learning outcomes, assessment discipline, verification.

d3.title: Professional Practice
d3.text: Documented practitioner activity, applied research, real-world deployment.

d4.title: Transparency & Cooperation
d4.text: Publication openness, international cooperation, digital institutional clarity.

index.note: Category weights are indicative, not final. Final weights will be published in the public Institute Index v1.0 methodology after AAB review.

analytics.disclaimer: WPA indexes are specialized analytical tools. Final scores are based on publicly verifiable evidence, methodological transparency and, where appropriate, human review by WPA and external advisors.

methodology.label: Methodological groups
methodology.title: Categories for fair comparison
methodology.lead: WPA will not mix small protocol academies with major universities in one raw table. Comparison is category-based, with clear inclusion criteria per group.

gA.name: Protocol / Etiquette / Diplomatic Protocol Academies
gA.examples: Protocol Academy of Macedonia, IEPA London, Accademia del Cerimoniale, Protocol School of Washington

gB.name: Diplomatic Academies and Government Training Institutes
gB.examples: Diplomatic Academy of Vienna, Bulgarian Diplomatic Institute, Diplomacy Academy Türkiye, Clingendael Institute

gC.name: Universities / Schools of Diplomacy, IR, Law and Political Sciences
gC.examples: China Foreign Affairs University, Sciences Po, CEDS France, College of Europe

gD.name: Foreign Service Institutes
gD.examples: US FSI, Instituto Rio Branco, ISEN Argentina, Foreign Service Institute of India, FSI Indonesia

gE.name: Cultural & Cross-cultural Diplomacy Institutes
gE.examples: Institute for Cultural Diplomacy, Anna Lindh Foundation, British Council Cultural Relations

gF.name: International Organization Training Centres
gF.examples: UN Staff College, OSCE Academy, NATO School, EU College of Europe (Bruges/Natolin)

gG.name: Public Relations and Communication Studies
gG.examples: USC Annenberg, NYU SPS, University of Amsterdam, NTU Wee Kim Wee School

gH.name: Security, Defence and Strategic Studies
gH.examples: NATO Defense College, King's College War Studies, Georgetown SSP, ANU SDSC

gI.name: Global Prestige Benchmark Institutions
gI.examples: Georgetown SFS, Johns Hopkins SAIS, Harvard Kennedy School, Oxford, LSE

benchmark.label: Global benchmark map
benchmark.title: Global map of benchmark institutions
benchmark.lead: The map is not a final ranking. It is an initial analytical map of selected institutions used as reference points for developing the WPA methodology.
benchmark.disclaimer: For public use, WPA uses only verified names and clean labels. Names with incorrect labels, duplicates or inaccurate country references will not be published.

opc.title: First International Conference on Protocol, Diplomacy, Public Relations & Security
opc.text1: OPC 2026 (Ohrid Protocol Conference) is the Institute's flagship event — gathering academic researchers, protocol officers, diplomats, security experts and communicators from over 17 countries.
opc.text2: The conference connects theory with practice, academic authority with field experience, and regional perspective with global standards.

practitioners.label: Practitioner lecture series
practitioners.title: Theory connected with practice
practitioners.lead: The WPA Institute invites experienced practitioners to deliver specialized lectures from their field. With written consent, lectures may be recorded and included in the WPA educational archive for students, participants, researchers and professionals.

c1.title: Protocol and diplomacy
c1.text: Chiefs of protocol, diplomats, former ambassadors, diplomatic officers, masters of ceremony and experts in state and international events.

c2.title: Defence, security and military attachés
c2.text: Defence and military attachés, security coordinators and experts in public events, crisis procedures, institutional security and inter-institutional coordination.

c3.title: Public communication, media and PR
c3.text: Spokespersons, PR directors, journalists, editors, media advisors and experts in crisis communication, public appearance and institutional reputation.

c4.title: Visual diplomacy and documentation
c4.text: Official photographers, camera teams, visual documentarians, translators, interpreters and professionals who shape the public image of diplomatic and protocol events.

s1.title: Invitation
s1.text: The Institute identifies an experienced practitioner based on a publicly documented position and role.

s2.title: Agreement
s2.text: Written consent is signed for topic, scope and right to record and archive.

s3.title: Lecture
s3.text: Lecture in a structured format: 45–60 min talk + 15 min Q&A with participants.

s4.title: Archive
s4.text: The lecture is added to the WPA educational archive with clear attribution and metadata.

s5.title: Recognition
s5.text: The practitioner receives WPA recognition for documented professional contribution — without academic title.

practitioners.disclaimer: Participation as a WPA practitioner lecturer does not represent an academic title, state accreditation or professional licence. It is a recognition of documented professional experience and contribution to the Institute's educational mission.

aab.label: Academic Advisory Board
aab.title: AAB — independent external advisory
aab.subtitle: Structure and role
aab.p1: The Academic Advisory Board (AAB) is an independent advisory body composed of distinguished academic and professional figures in the Institute's domain: professors, former diplomats, chiefs of protocol, security experts and communication specialists.
aab.p2: The AAB reviews Institute Index methodology, verifies the quality of working papers, advises on research priorities and provides independent opinion in cases with potential conflict of interest.
aab.p3: The AAB is not an operational body — the Institute is led by the Director. The AAB is an advisory conscience: a check on quality, not on operations.

publications.label: Public outputs
publications.title: Publications and analytical outputs of the Institute

s1.title: WPA Working Papers
s1.text: Preliminary research papers and analyses open for discussion.

s2.title: WPA Protocol Studies Review
s2.text: Specialized analyses in protocol and diplomatic practice.

s3.title: WPA Policy Briefs
s3.text: Concise applied recommendations for institutions and professionals.

s4.title: WPA Case Studies
s4.text: Documented case studies in the WPA Institute field.

s5.title: WPA Annual Report
s5.text: Annual institutional review of activities, results and future priorities.

s6.title: WPA Global Benchmark
s6.text: Specialized benchmark for protocol, diplomacy, communication and security.

cta.title: The Institute is building a serious, modern, internationally oriented academy — with structure, dignity and clear direction.

footer.about_text: Institute for Protocol, Diplomacy, Public Communication & Security Studies. Institutional formula: theory + practice + ethics + measurement.
footer.bottom: © 2026 World Protocol Academy · Institute for Protocol, Diplomacy, Public Communication & Security Studies · Negotiation is optional. Protocol is absolute.
